name: HTTPS Enforcement
description: Verifies the page is served over HTTPS and sends a Strict-Transport-Security header. HTTPS is a confirmed Google ranking signal and a baseline trust requirement for AI crawlers; HSTS prevents protocol downgrade attacks.

criteria.pass: URL scheme is `https://` AND response includes `Strict-Transport-Security` header
criteria.warn: URL scheme is `https://` but no HSTS header
criteria.fail: URL scheme is `http://` or page loads over HTTP

fix_template: Redirect HTTP → HTTPS at server level. Add header: `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains`
sources: Google confirmed HTTPS as ranking signal (2014). HSTS prevents downgrade attacks. All AI crawlers require HTTPS for trust signals.

name: Title Tag Present, 50-60 Characters
description: Verifies a <title> tag exists and its length falls in the 50-60 character optimum. Google truncates titles at ~600px (~60 chars), and titles under 50 chars are usually too generic to differentiate in SERPs.

criteria.fail: No `<title>` tag OR title empty OR title < 40 characters OR title > 70 characters

sources: Google truncates at ~600px (~60 chars). 2026 consensus: 50-60 is optimal. Titles < 50 are usually too generic to differentiate in SERPs. API leak confirmed `titlematchScore`. Pixel width (600px) is the real limit, not character count.

name: Meta Description Present, 120-160 Characters
description: Verifies a meta description exists at 120-160 characters. Not a ranking signal, but pages with custom descriptions get ~25% higher CTR than auto-generated snippets.

criteria.fail: No meta description OR empty OR > 180 characters

fix_template: `<meta name="description" content="[Entity] is a [category] that [key benefit]. [Proof point or CTA]. [Differentiator].">`
sources: Not a ranking signal (confirmed by Google). Affects CTR — pages with custom descriptions get ~25% higher CTR than auto-generated snippets. Google may rewrite descriptions regardless.

name: Canonical Tag Present and Self-Referencing
description: Verifies a canonical link element exists and points at the current page URL (after normalization). Canonical is the primary URL-consolidation signal; missing or cross-domain canonicals fragment siteAuthority.

criteria.pass: `<link rel="canonical" href="[current URL]">` exists AND href matches current page URL (normalized — trailing slashes, www/non-www)
criteria.warn: Canonical exists but points to a different URL on same domain (intentional consolidation possible)
criteria.fail: No canonical tag OR canonical points to different domain OR canonical is empty

sources: Google API leak confirmed `siteAuthority` — split domains fragment this signal. Canonical is the primary signal for URL consolidation. Missing canonical on redirecting domains (e.g., trypsagent.com → jointryps.com) is a critical failure.

name: Robots Meta Allows Indexing
description: Verifies the page is not marked noindex/none via the robots meta tag. A noindex directive excludes the page from the Google index and from AI Overviews regardless of any other signal.

criteria.pass: No `<meta name="robots">` tag (default = index) OR tag contains `index` or `all`

fix_template: Remove `noindex` or change to `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow, max-snippet:-1, max-image-preview:large">`
sources: Google Rule #1166 (0.99): "Noindex directive excludes page from Google index regardless of other signals." Also excludes from AI Overviews.
notes: This is a GATE CHECK — if this fails, Gate 1 triggers and dominates the report

name: Exactly One H1 Tag
description: Verifies the page has exactly one H1 element. The H1 signals the primary topic to search engines and AI extractors; a single H1 is best practice for clarity.

criteria.warn: 0 H1 tags (missing) or 2 H1 tags (might be intentional in HTML5 sectioning)

fix_template:
Ensure single `

[Primary page topic]

` early in content
sources: Google has said multiple H1s are "fine" but single H1 is best practice for clarity. The H1 signals the primary topic to search engines and AI extractors.

name: H1 Contains Primary Keyword
description: Verifies the H1 text contains the primary keyword or a close semantic variant of the target query. AI extractors use the H1 as the section anchor for topic identification.
method_detail: Compare H1 text to title/query keywords

criteria.pass: H1 text contains the primary keyword or a close semantic variant from the target query
criteria.warn: H1 is related but doesn't contain the keyword (e.g., branded tagline)
criteria.fail: H1 is completely unrelated to target query/page topic

fix_template:
Rewrite H1 to include primary keyword naturally: `

[Keyword-rich descriptive heading]

`
sources: API leak: `titlematchScore` tracks title-query relevance. H1 serves similar function for on-page topic signaling. AI extractors use H1 as section anchor.

name: HTML Lang Attribute
description: Verifies the <html> element declares a lang attribute. A minor signal that helps search engines and AI systems determine content language and is required for accessibility (WCAG).

criteria.fail: No lang attribute on html tag

sources: Minor signal. Helps search engines and AI systems determine content language. Required for accessibility (WCAG). Google uses visible content for language detection (not lang attr), but absence is a quality gap.

name: Viewport Meta Tag
description: Verifies the viewport meta tag is present. Required for mobile-first indexing — without it the page renders as desktop on mobile, risking ranking suppression.
sources: Required for mobile-first indexing. Google crawls mobile version primarily. Without viewport, page renders as desktop on mobile — poor experience, potential ranking suppression.

name: robots.txt Allows Crawling
description: Verifies robots.txt does not Disallow the audited page path for Googlebot or the wildcard user-agent. A Disallow prevents crawling entirely, which prevents indexing (distinct from noindex).
method_detail: Parse robots.txt Disallow rules for page path

criteria.pass: robots.txt has no Disallow rule covering the audited page path for Googlebot or wildcard
criteria.warn: robots.txt returns 404 (no file — treated as fully permissive by crawlers, but missing is not intentional)
criteria.fail: robots.txt Disallows the audited page path for Googlebot or wildcard User-agent

fix_template: Remove the Disallow line or add specific Allow for the page path
sources: Googlebot Disallow = page will not be indexed. Different from noindex (Disallow prevents crawling; noindex prevents indexing after crawling).
notes: This is a GATE CHECK — blocking Googlebot triggers Gate 1

name: Sitemap Referenced
description: Verifies a sitemap is referenced from robots.txt or the HTML head. Sitemaps help crawlers discover pages efficiently; lastmod is the only sitemap field Google uses.
method_detail: Check robots.txt Sitemap: + link rel sitemap

criteria.pass: robots.txt contains `Sitemap: [URL]` directive OR `<link rel="sitemap" href="...">` in HTML head
criteria.warn: Neither present but sitemap exists at /sitemap.xml
criteria.fail: No sitemap reference anywhere AND no sitemap.xml found

sources: Sitemaps help crawlers discover pages efficiently. Google ignores `priority` and `changefreq` (confirmed Rule #562, 0.99). lastmod is the only useful sitemap signal.

name: Page Renders Without JS Dependency
description: Measures how much body text is present in the raw HTML response. GPTBot, PerplexityBot, and ClaudeBot do not execute JavaScript, so content behind JS is invisible to AI crawlers.
method_detail: WebFetch content volume vs Chrome (if available)

criteria.pass: WebFetch returns > 500 words of body text (content is in raw HTML)
criteria.fail: WebFetch returns < 200 words of body text (heavily JS-dependent)

fix_template: Implement server-side rendering (SSR) or static site generation (SSG)
sources: GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot do NOT execute JavaScript (Vercel 500M request study: zero JS execution by GPTBot). Content behind JS is invisible to AI crawlers. Google renders JS but with delay.
notes: This is a GATE CHECK (Gate 2) when combined with Chrome MCP unavailability
